overview: Azorius Control is a top-tier Pioneer deck that leverages Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion to empower creature-based strategies. It adopts a reactive, backwards posture focused on countering spells, removing threats, and exhausting the opponent's resources before deploying powerful late-game win conditions.

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction like Censor or Lay Down Arms
Hands containing proper answers tailored to the specific matchup
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early counterspells or removal
Hands without enough lands to support a heavy curve

examples.reason: Provides early interaction and lands to stabilize, aligning with the deck's heavy curve and explicit advice to mulligan for proper answers.

early_game: Play lands to support a heavy curve of 4+ mana spells
Use early counterspells like Censor as a Force Spike
Remove small creatures with Lay Down Arms
mid_game: Stabilize by countering key threats and using board wipes like Supreme Verdict or Farewell
Exhaust opponent's resources while maintaining card advantage with Memory Deluge
late_game: Deploy win conditions like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and The Wandering Emperor
Attack with manlands or Regal Caracal tokens
Finish with Shark Typhoon or Starnheim Unleashed

key_cards.name: Kaheera, the Orphanguard
key_cards.notes: Provides +1/+1 to Cat creatures, enabling the Regal Caracal strategy and requiring two white mana to cast.

key_cards.name: Lay Down Arms
key_cards.notes: Main removal that synergizes with Plains-type lands and handles both small and large creatures.

key_cards.name: Dovin’s Veto
key_cards.notes: Mighty counter against Midranges and in the mirror match.

key_cards.name: Make Disappear
key_cards.notes: Versatile counter that synergizes with creature tokens the deck can generate.

key_cards.name: Change the Equation
key_cards.notes: Indispensable in the current meta and extremely versatile.

key_cards.name: Memory Deluge
key_cards.notes: Main engine that fetches needed parts at the most appropriate time.

key_cards.name: Regal Caracal
key_cards.notes: Sideboard wincon that creates two cat tokens, heavily powered by Kaheera.

sequencing_tips: Play backwards by responding to opponent threats before deploying your own
Save answers in mirror matches and avoid rushing to prevent fatal mistakes
Prioritize countering Storm the Festival and preventing Karn from entering the board against Nykthos Ramp
Do not save removal against Gruul Midrange; kill elves immediately to prevent explosive damage

matchups.advice: Keep threats under control and ensure breath in game 1. Post-side, use Starnheim Unleashed to evade discard and Regal Caracal for token pressure.
Kill Greasefang and Okiba Boss early, counter graveyard-filling spells, and use board wipes. Rest in Peace is key post-side.
Open with many counterspells, destroy Nykthos with Field of Ruin, keep devotion near zero, and never let Karn resolve.
Do not save removal; kill elves immediately to prevent explosive damage. Finish with planeswalkers or Shark Typhoon post-side.
Play a cold war, save answers, and avoid mistakes. Resolve Emperor on the pass with backup counterspells. Shark Typhoon evades counters.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Azorius Control is a classic representative of the Control archetype... Kaheera Control, a version of Azorius that runs Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and The Wandering Emperor are an almost unbeatable duo
Shark Typhoon is also very versatile and can either give you that extra draw or put a 5/5 creature with flying into play
Regal Caracal is a very interesting addition to the deck's strategy
Starnheim Unleashed is another alternative wincon
Hall of Storm Giants is another possible wincondition
the deck can still use manlands to end the game in just a few turns

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: our posture here is to play 'backwards', responding to the opponent's threats and controlling the game until we stabilize
Play it cool, save your answers. In this matchup, managing to resolve an Emperor on the pass with a backup counterspell can be decisive
counter Storm the Festival. Under no circumstances should you allow Karn to join the board.
Don't leave the elves alive and don't save removals

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Reckoner Bankbuster, essential cards in the strategy of Azorius' main rival, Rakdos Midrange
Killing Greasefang, Okiba Boss and countering the spells that feed the graveyard is essential.
counter Storm the Festival. Under no circumstances should you allow Karn to join the board.
Classic versions of Azorius have more countespells in the main deck or run Yorion, Sky Nomad, and sometimes also have the Narset, Parter of Veils + Day's Undoing combo.
Hallowed Moonlight is a card that will act as a removal against decks with Creativity and Transmogrify.
